title: celtis mississippiensis var. pumila
intro: Celtis mississippiensis var. pumila, also known as the dwarf hackberry or dwarf sugarberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. The plant is said to have medicinal properties, with its bark and leaves containing tannins and other compounds that have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Celtis mississippiensis var. pumila include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as fever and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Celtis mississippiensis var. pumila include infusions and decoctions made from its bark, leaves, and roots, which can be consumed as teas or used in topical applications.
